pr_body: Adds ping_host so operators can ping a caller-supplied host from the dashboard.
author: contributor

files.patch:
@@ -1 +1,5 @@
 import subprocess
+
+
+def ping_host(host):
+    return subprocess.Popen(["bash", "-c", "ping -c1 " + host]).wait()

triage: {"file_tiers": {"ops/net.py": "deep"}, "overall_risk": "high", "relevant_dimensions": ["security"], "reasoning": "True-positive scenario (command_injection recall, single-finding, JUDGED-only): ping_host concatenates a caller-supplied host into a 'bash -c' command run via subprocess.Popen list-form. There is no shell=True keyword and no os.system call, so the OBSERVED structural queries do not fire -- this is a model-dependent (JUDGED) command injection."}

Pass the host as an argv element to a fixed binary (no shell), or validate it against a strict allowlist.", "evidence": "    return subprocess.Popen([\"bash\", \"-c\", \"ping -c1 \" + host]).wait()", "line_start": 5, "line_end": 5, "trace_candidates": []}]}
synthesize: True-positive: one command_injection finding -- ping_host runs a caller-supplied host through 'bash -c', so the caller can execute arbitrary commands; the list-form Popen dodges the OBSERVED shell=True query, so this is the JUDGED path.
